codeReview.summary: The feature does not fully implement the contract. The async additions only unit-test the helper primitives in isolation rather than wiring them into a real async-sensitive path, and the browser proof added under the committed feature used a non-canonical `.real-runtime.spec.ts` filename that directly hard-coded URLs instead of following the repository's E2E helper pattern.

issues.description: The new async tests only exercise `testsupport.NewTestQueueConfig()` and `DrainHelper` with synthetic goroutines / a bare `JobRunner`; they never wire the helpers into an existing async-sensitive backend path. The feature contract required at least one real async-sensitive test path to concretely use queue prefix, worker ownership, and drain-to-idle behavior, so this remains documentation-level proof rather than implementation coverage.

The committed browser smoke proof landed under a `.real-runtime.spec.ts` filename even though mission guidance and `.factory/library/testing-lanes.md` say new E2E files must use normal `*.spec.ts` names because E2E already implies real runtime. This violated the canonical lane contract and later had to be renamed in a follow-up feature, so the reviewed feature did not actually leave the browser lane in the required final state.

The smoke spec hard-codes `http://localhost:5173` URLs and manually performs registration instead of using the existing E2E auth helpers (`apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts`) that already resolve the configured base URL and assert shell readiness. Reusing the helpers would better match repository conventions and avoid duplicating setup logic.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `test-platform-worker` skill tells workers to use `agent-browser` for a real browser smoke check when touching browser-visible runtime behavior, but this worker's transcript shows no `Skill(agent-browser)` invocation and relied only on Playwright file creation plus `--list` output.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill: .factory/skills/test-platform-worker/SKILL.md step 5; transcript skeleton for worker session ca3e4229-a0c1-4805-a69a-64c0e834e71a contains no agent-browser tool call; handoff verification only reports `vp run test:e2e:website -- --list`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repository already has shared Playwright auth helpers for registration/login/session bootstrap, but the worker reimplemented those steps inline in the smoke test instead of following the established pattern. This convention is not captured in shared mission state.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Existing helper: apps/website/e2e/fixtures/e2e-auth.ts; committed spec in git show 61f3356 adds direct form-fill/fetch logic instead of calling `registerE2eUser` or `readSessionBootstrap`.

summary: Reviewed commit 61f3356 and the worker transcript/handoff. Fails review because the async coverage is only helper-level synthetic testing rather than a real async-sensitive path, and the browser proof was committed under the non-canonical `.real-runtime.spec.ts` naming that the mission explicitly forbids for new E2E files.
